{
  "packet_id": "module__gpio_regs__function_model",
  "owner_module": "gpio_regs",
  "owner_file": "rtl/gpio_regs.sv",
  "todo_plan_sha256": "123947ae1f7d9f96f7ef25a8af2118a8e5c5ac198b54689b9c8f4223188b5dce",
  "implemented_sources": [
    "function_model.state_variables.dir_state",
    "function_model.state_variables.dout_state",
    "function_model.state_variables.din_state",
    "function_model.transactions.FM1_LATCH_CONTROL",
    "function_model.transactions.FM2_SAMPLE_INPUTS",
    "function_model.transactions.FM4_ASYNC_RESET",
    "function_model.transactions.FM1_LATCH_CONTROL.output_rules.dir_q_next",
    "function_model.transactions.FM1_LATCH_CONTROL.output_rules.dout_q_next",
    "function_model.transactions.FM2_SAMPLE_INPUTS.output_rules.din_q_masked_next"
  ],
  "notes": [
    "Module implements reset and sequential sampling semantics from SSOT function_model and cycle_model.",
    "FM3 combinational pad drive is intentionally left for gpio_pad_logic owner module."
  ]
}

Implemented initial gpio_regs owner RTL for function_model slice.

Key implemented behavior:
- Async active-low reset clears dir_q/dout_q/din_q to zero (FM4).
- Rising-edge control latch of dir_in->dir_q and dout_in->dout_q (FM1).
- Rising-edge masked input sample din_q <= (din_q & dir_q) | (pad_in & ~dir_q) (FM2).

Design intent:
- Keep state-owner logic in one sequential block for atomic update visibility.
- Use WIDTH parameter for all vector sizing.
- Avoid loops/functions/packages per project RTL subset.

Follow-up integration consideration:
- If later packet enforces strict gpio_regs port contract without pad_in/din_q, move FM2 state update to gpio_input_sampler and keep gpio_regs focused on dir_q/dout_q only.
